tool.name: RMSX trajectory analysis
tool.command: mkdir -p rmsx_output pdb_slices && #if str($input_source.source) == "example" ln -s '$__tool_directory__/test-data/1UBQ.pdb' input_topology.pdb && ln -s '$__tool_directory__/test-data/mon_sys.dcd' input_trajectory.dcd && topology_path='input_topology.pdb' && trajectory_path='input_trajectory.dcd' && #else ln -s '$input_source.topology' 'input_topology.$input_source.topology.ext' && ln -s '$input_source.trajectory' 'input_trajectory.$input_source.trajectory.ext' && topology_path='input_topology.$input_source.topology.ext' && trajectory_path='input_trajectory.$input_source.trajectory.ext' && #end if chain_id='$input_source.chain' && python3 '$__tool_directory__/rmsx_preflight.py' --topology "\${topology_path}" --trajectory "\${trajectory_path}" --selector "\${chain_id}" --num-slices '$input_source.num_slices' --start-frame '$analysis.start_frame' #if str($analysis.end_frame).strip() not in ("", "None") --end-frame '$analysis.end_frame' #end if --analysis-type '$analysis.analysis_type' > '$log' 2>&1 && RMSX_NO_CITATION=1 rmsx "\${topology_path}" "\${trajectory_path}" --output_dir rmsx_output --num_slices '$input_source.num_slices' --chain "\${chain_id}" --palette viridis --start_frame '$analysis.start_frame' #if str($analysis.end_frame).strip() not in ("", "None") --end_frame '$analysis.end_frame' #end if --analysis_type '$analysis.analysis_type' --summary_n '$analysis.summary_n' $analysis.interpolate --no-plot --quiet --overwrite >> '$log' 2>&1 && chain_dir="rmsx_output/chain_\${chain_id}_rmsx" && source_rmsx_csv=\$(python3 -c "import glob, sys; matches = sorted(glob.glob(sys.argv[1] + '/rmsx_*.csv')); print(matches[0]) if matches else sys.exit('No RMSX CSV found in ' + sys.argv[1])" "\${chain_dir}") && cp "\${source_rmsx_csv}" '$rmsx_table' && cp "\${chain_dir}"/rmsd.csv '$rmsd_table' && cp "\${chain_dir}"/rmsf.csv '$rmsf_table' && if [ -f "\${chain_dir}"/masked_residues.csv ]; then cp "\${chain_dir}"/masked_residues.csv '$mask_table'; else python3 -c "import csv, sys; rows = csv.DictReader(open(sys.argv[1], newline='')); out = csv.DictWriter(open(sys.argv[2], 'w', newline=''), fieldnames=['ResidueID', 'ChainID', 'Masked'], lineterminator='\n'); out.writeheader(); out.writerows({'ResidueID': row['ResidueID'], 'ChainID': row['ChainID'], 'Masked': 'False'} for row in rows)" '$rmsx_table' '$mask_table'; fi && cp "\${chain_dir}"/slice_*_first_frame.pdb pdb_slices/ && python3 '$__tool_directory__/rmsx_static_plots.py' --rmsx-source "\${source_rmsx_csv}" --rmsd-source "\${chain_dir}/rmsd.csv" --rmsf-source "\${chain_dir}/rmsf.csv" --palette '$palette' --heatmap-output '$rmsx_heatmap_plot' --triple-output '$rmsx_triple_plot' #if str($analysis.interpolate) == "--interpolate" --interpolate #end if >> '$log' 2>&1 && python3 '$__tool_directory__/rmsx_molstar_report.py' --pdb-dir pdb_slices --rmsx-table '$rmsx_table' --mask-table '$mask_table' --palette '$palette' --manifest-output '$viewer_manifest' --title 'RMSX Molstar FlipBook viewer'

param.name: topology
param.label: Topology or structure file
param.help: A topology/structure readable by MDAnalysis and RMSX, such as PDB, PSF, PRMTOP, or GRO.

param.name: trajectory
param.label: Trajectory file
param.help: A trajectory readable by MDAnalysis and RMSX, such as DCD, XTC, or TRR.

param.name: chain
param.label: Chain/segment selector
param.help: Passed to RMSX with --chain. Comma-separated values such as A,B are allowed; the preflight check reports available segment IDs and chain IDs.

validator.message: Use comma-separated letters, numbers, underscores, hyphens, or periods.

param.name: chain
param.label: Chain/segment selector
param.help: The bundled 1UBQ/mon_sys example uses segment ID 7.

validator.message: Use comma-separated letters, numbers, underscores, hyphens, or periods.

param.name: start_frame
param.label: Start frame
param.help: Zero-based first trajectory frame to include.

param.name: end_frame
param.label: End frame
param.help: Optional zero-based last trajectory frame to include. Leave blank to analyze through the final frame.

param.name: analysis_type
param.label: Analysis type
param.help: Passed to RMSX with --analysis_type. The default protein mode matches the current RMSX/FlipBook path.

param.name: summary_n
param.label: Summary residue count
param.help: Number of top-changing residues RMSX should include in its summary outputs.

param.name: interpolate
param.label: Interpolate RMSX slice values
param.help: Disabled by default to preserve the current validated wrapper behavior.

param.name: palette
param.label: Molstar and plot color palette
param.help: Initial color ramp used by the native Galaxy Molstar FlipBook visualization and the static RMSX heatmap/triple plots.

data.name: viewer_manifest
data.label: ${tool.name} on ${on_string}: Molstar native viewer manifest - open with Visualize

tool.help:
.. class:: infomark
**What it does**
RMSX partitions a molecular dynamics trajectory into equal time slices and computes per-residue RMSF within each slice. The resulting table shows where and when residue-level motion changes over the trajectory.
This Galaxy wrapper is intentionally RMSX-only. It does not launch FlipBook, ChimeraX, or VMD. It exposes the RMSX compute outputs and adds a native Galaxy Molstar FlipBook viewer manifest for inspecting the generated PDB slices through Galaxy's Visualize action.
_____
.. class:: infomark
**Inputs**
The tool can either use datasets from the current history or load the bundled 1UBQ/mon_sys example data. The example path uses the same fixtures as the wrapper tests and defaults to segment ID ``7`` with three slices.
For history inputs, topology/structure and trajectory files are passed through to RMSX with their Galaxy file extensions preserved. RMSX uses MDAnalysis, so common molecular dynamics pairs such as PDB/DCD, PSF/DCD, PRMTOP/NetCDF, GRO/XTC, and related MDAnalysis-readable formats are the intended target. The wrapper runs a preflight check before RMSX starts so load failures, impossible frame windows, and missing chain/segment selectors produce clearer Galaxy logs.
The chain/segment selector is passed to RMSX with ``--chain`` and may contain a comma-separated list such as ``A,B``. The preflight log reports the segment IDs and chain IDs it found in the topology to make selector problems easier to diagnose.
The RMSX options section exposes start frame, optional end frame, analysis type, summary residue count, and interpolation. Interpolation is used for the static RMSX heatmap raster. The selected palette is shared by the static R plots and the native Molstar FlipBook viewer so the heatmap and 3D view can be compared directly.
_____
.. class:: infomark
**Outputs**
The tool produces:
* RMSX CSV table with one row per residue and one column per time slice. * RMSD CSV over the analyzed frame window. * RMSF CSV over the analyzed frame window. * Mask metadata CSV. The current CLI path does not expose masking, so this will normally mark residues as unmasked. * PDB slice collection with RMSX values written into the B-factor column. * RMSX heatmap PNG generated with the original RMSX R plotting script. * RMSX triple-plot PNG combining RMSD, RMSX heatmap, and RMSF panels. * Native Molstar viewer manifest for Galaxy's Visualize path. * Execution log.
The native viewer manifest embeds the current PDB slices and RMSX display metadata using schema version ``rmsx-molstar-viewer/v1`` and is emitted as the project-local ``rmsxmolstar`` datatype. That datatype subclasses Galaxy's JSON datatype, so the manifest remains structured JSON while gaining a dedicated Visualize association. Install the accompanying ``rmsx_molstar`` Galaxy visualization plugin to open the manifest as the interactive Molstar FlipBook viewer without using trusted HTML outputs.
